title: AppVersion
content:
# AppVersion


## Properties

- **name** (`string`): The name of the application. This is the unique identifier for the application.
- **title** (`string`): Human-readable name of the application.
- **description** (`string`): Human-readable description of the application.
- **category** (`string`): The category of the application.
- **type** (`string`): The type of application.

- `app`: An App that allows mapping inputs and outputs to data
streams, sending control changes, recommendations, and data tags.
- `importer`: Connects to an external system to import data into the
platform as well as receive control changes to act on the external
system.
- `exporter`: Connects to the platform to export data to an external
system.
- `docker`: An external application that does not connect to the
platform's data streams.
- `task`: An application type used internally by the Tasks feature.
- **version** (`string`): The version of the application being created.
- **flags** (`object`): Flags that can be set to enable specific behaviours on the platform when managing the application.
- **io** (`array`): 
- **dynamic_io** (`array`): 
- **parameters** (`array`): 
- **custom_actions** (`array`): 
- **data_quality** (`array`):
summary: Object with properties: name, title, description, category, type

name.description: The name of the application. This is the unique identifier for the application.

type.description:
The type of application.

- `app`: An App that allows mapping inputs and outputs to data
streams, sending control changes, recommendations, and data tags.
- `importer`: Connects to an external system to import data into the
platform as well as receive control changes to act on the external
system.
- `exporter`: Connects to the platform to export data to an external
system.
- `docker`: An external application that does not connect to the
platform's data streams.
- `task`: An application type used internally by the Tasks feature.

version.description: The version of the application being created.

flags.description: Flags that can be set to enable specific behaviours on the platform when managing the application.

spec_version.description: The version of the specification that the application is using.

legacy_data_types.description: If `true`, the application uses the legacy data types (e.g. `raw.float64`, `raw.int32`,...).

gateway_mode.description: The mode which the gateway is operates on. The default is `socket`, which uses a socket as an interface for the application to connect to the realtime data flow.

resources_required.description: Define whether the application is deployed to specific resources (e.g. assets) or not. Applications that have inputs or outputs always require resources.

name.description: The name of the input/output used in the Application.

data_type.description: Primitive data types are: `string`, `number`, `boolean` and `object`. If another name is used, it will be considered an object with that name. It's highly recommended using a custom name when using objects.

way.description: The direction and characteristics of the input/output. The `cc` suffix means that in accepts or sends Control Changes on the input or output respectively.

storage.description: The storage location of the data. `node-and-cloud` means that the data is stored in the node and in the cloud. `node` means that the data is stored only in the node. `none` means that the data is not stored.

unit.description: The unit of the data. It's recommended to use the [UCUM](http://unitsofmeasure.org/ucum.html) standard for units.

items.description: Primitive data types are: `string`, `number`, `boolean` and `object`. If another name is used, it will be considered an object with that name. It's highly recommended using a custom name when using objects.

data_types.description: List of data types that the dynamic I/O type can have. If empty, any data type is allowed.

data_type.description: The data type of the parameter. It can be `string`, `number`, or `boolean`.

type.description: The type of the custom action.

way.description: The direction of the custom action. If `input-ca`, the app recieves and executes custom actions. If `output-ca`, the app sends custom actions which are then executed by an app with the corresponding `input-ca`.

name.description: The name of the data quality.

data_type.description: Primitive data types are: `string`, `number`, `boolean` and `object`. If another name is used, it will be considered an object with that name. It's highly recommended using a custom name when using objects.

way.description: The direction of the data quality. If `output`, the app computes and produces the data quality value. If `input`, the app receives the data quality value.
